summary: Extend mock classes in selection.test.ts: add `sub(v)`, `multiplyScalar(s)` to MockVector3; add `getWorldDirection(target)` to MockPerspectiveCamera. These are needed for V-02/V-03 tests but should be added upfront to avoid mixing mock infrastructure changes with test logic.; V-10: Write failing tests for click-vs-drag discrimination in selection.test.ts. New describe block 'click-vs-drag discrimination' with tests: (1) pointerdown alone does NOT fire onSelect, (2) pointerdown+pointerup at same position fires onSelect with raycasted entity, (3) pointerdown+pointerup with >5px movement does NOT fire onSelect, (4) pointerdown+pointerup with <=5px movement fires onSelect, (5) onSelect receives null when pointerup raycast misses, (6) after dispose, pointerup does not fire onSelect.; V-10: Implement click-vs-drag discrimination in selection.ts. Replace `handlePointerDown` (which fires onSelect immediately) with: (a) `handlePointerDown` that records {clientX, clientY} and adds a one-shot pointerup listener, (b) `handlePointerUp` that computes Euclidean distance from pointerdown position and only calls raycast()+onSelect() if distance < 5px. Update dispose() to also remove pointerup listener. Update existing 'click-based selection raycasting' tests and 'rAF-throttled pointermove / pointerdown still raycasts synchronously' test to dispatch both pointerdown+pointerup events instead of just pointerdown.; V-02/V-03: Write failing tests for fitToView with view-direction offset and controls.target update. In selection.test.ts, add tests: (1) fitToView offsets camera along current view direction (not just Z) — set camera at an angle, verify position is center - viewDir*distance, (2) fitToView updates controls.target to bounding box center when controls provided via SelectionOptions, (3) fitToView still works without controls (backward compat). Update setup() to optionally accept a controls mock with a target Vector3.; V-02/V-03: Fix fitToView in selection.ts. Add optional `controls?: { target: { copy: (v: any) => void } }` field to SelectionOptions interface. In fitToView(): (a) get view direction via `camera.getWorldDirection(dir)`, (b) position camera at `center - viewDir * distance` instead of just Z offset, (c) if controls provided, set `controls.target.copy(center)`. Update existing fitToView tests that asserted Z-only offset to match new view-direction behavior.; V-02/V-03: Write failing tests for flyToEntity with view-direction offset and controls.target update. Tests: (1) flyToEntity offsets camera along current view direction, (2) flyToEntity updates controls.target to entity bounding box center, (3) flyToEntity with unknown entity does not crash or update controls.target.; V-02/V-03: Fix flyToEntity in selection.ts. Apply same view-direction offset and controls.target update pattern as fitToView. Update existing flyToEntity tests that asserted Z-only offset.

Completed final two steps: step-7 test was already written by prior iteration but uncommitted (confirmed it fails correctly), committed it. step-8 added `controls: controls.controls` to createSelection call in Viewport.tsx to thread OrbitControls through for orbit target updates. All 70 tests pass (56 selection + 14 Viewport).

Add three-mesh-bvh to gui/package.json dependencies and run npm install. This package provides computeBoundsTree/disposeBoundsTree for BufferGeometry BVH acceleration and acceleratedRaycast for Mesh.prototype.raycast. Required for P-01 (BVH raycasting).; P-03 render-on-demand: Write failing tests in Viewport.test.tsx. Update controls mock to include addEventListener/removeEventListener on the controls object (currently just {}). Add test: 'animate loop does NOT call renderer.render when idle after initial frame' — use capturing rAF mock, fire first rAF callback (initial render), clear renderer.render mock, fire second rAF callback with no interactions, assert renderer.render was NOT called. This test fails against current code which always renders every frame.; P-03 render-on-demand: Implement in Viewport.tsx. Add let needsRender = true flag. Add requestRender() helper that sets needsRender = true. Register controls.controls.addEventListener('change', requestRender) to mark dirty on camera movement. In animate loop: keep calling controls.update() every frame (required for enableDamping), but guard renderer.render(scene, camera) behind if(needsRender), then reset needsRender = false. Add controls.controls.removeEventListener('change', requestRender) in onCleanup. Initial needsRender = true ensures first frame renders.; P-03 render triggers: Write tests verifying all re-render triggers. (1) 'controls change event triggers re-render on next frame' — fire controls mock's captured 'change' callback, then fire rAF, assert renderer.render called. (2) 'resize triggers re-render' — verify renderer.render is called on next frame after ResizeObserver fires. (3) Test that renderer.render is still called once for initial frame (existing test 'animate loop does not call renderer.render after cleanup/dispose' should continue to pass).; P-03 render triggers: Wire requestRender() calls in Viewport.tsx at all trigger points. Add requestRender() after meshManager.sync(props.meshes) in the mesh sync createEffect. Add requestRender() after selection.setHovered() and selection.setSelected() in their createEffects. Add requestRender() after resize() call in the ResizeObserver callback (alongside existing invalidateRect). Verify all tests from step-3 pass plus existing tests remain green.; P-02 EdgesGeometry: Write failing test in selection.test.ts. Add MockEdgesGeometry to the three mock (identical structure to existing MockWireframeGeometry). Add test: 'setSelected creates EdgesGeometry from mesh geometry' — verify that when setSelected('A') is called, EdgesGeometry constructor is invoked with mesh.geometry and the resulting LineSegments is added to scene. Update existing wireframe tests to use EdgesGeometry mock if needed. This test fails because current code imports/uses WireframeGeometry.; P-02 EdgesGeometry: In selection.ts, change import from 'WireframeGeometry' to 'EdgesGeometry' in the three import statement. In setSelected(), replace 'new WireframeGeometry(mesh.geometry)' with 'new EdgesGeometry(mesh.geometry)'. EdgesGeometry is a direct drop-in replacement that produces LineSegments-compatible geometry but only includes edges where adjacent face normals differ by > 1 degree threshold, making it dramatically lighter for dense meshes while preserving the visual outline.; P-01 BVH geometry: Write failing tests in meshManager.test.ts. Add computeBoundsTree = vi.fn() and disposeBoundsTree = vi.fn() to MockBufferGeometry. Mock the 'three-mesh-bvh' module to export computeBoundsTree, disposeBoundsTree, acceleratedRaycast. Add tests: (1) 'sync calls geometry.computeBoundsTree() on newly created mesh', (2) 'sync calls geometry.computeBoundsTree() after updating existing mesh geometry', (3) 'removeMesh calls geometry.disposeBoundsTree() before disposing geometry'. These fail because current meshManager doesn't invoke BVH methods.; P-01 BVH geometry: In meshManager.ts, import { computeBoundsTree, disposeBoundsTree } from 'three-mesh-bvh' and BufferGeometry from 'three'. Apply prototype patches at module scope: BufferGeometry.prototype.computeBoundsTree = computeBoundsTree; BufferGeometry.prototype.disposeBoundsTree = disposeBoundsTree. In createMeshFromData(), call geometry.computeBoundsTree() after all attributes are set. In updateMeshGeometry(), call geometry.computeBoundsTree() after updating all attributes (rebuilds BVH for changed geometry). In removeMesh(), call (mesh.geometry as any).disposeBoundsTree() before geometry.dispose().; P-01 BVH raycasting: Write failing test in selection.test.ts. Add mock for 'three-mesh-bvh' module. Add test: 'raycaster has firstHitOnly set to true' — after createSelection, inspect the mock Raycaster instance to verify firstHitOnly property is true. This test fails because current code doesn't set firstHitOnly on the raycaster.

Completed final 3 steps. step-10: Implemented BVH-accelerated raycasting in selection.ts by importing acceleratedRaycast from three-mesh-bvh, patching Mesh.prototype.raycast, and setting firstHitOnly=true on raycaster. step-11: Added edge case test for sync({}) removing all meshes. step-12: Replaced Set allocation with 'in' operator for O(1) key lookup without allocation overhead. All 106 tests pass (58 selection + 17 meshManager + 19 Viewport + 5 controls + 7 scene).

P-01 BVH raycasting: In selection.ts, import { acceleratedRaycast } from 'three-mesh-bvh' and import Mesh from 'three'. Apply prototype patch at module scope: Mesh.prototype.raycast = acceleratedRaycast. After creating the Raycaster instance (line 42), set (raycaster as any).firstHitOnly = true. This enables BVH-accelerated raycasting that exits early on first hit, which is correct since we only use intersections[0].object.name.; P-04 sync optimization: Run existing meshManager tests to verify they all pass (behavior is unchanged, this is a pure refactor). Optionally add test: 'sync with empty incoming object removes all existing meshes' to ensure edge case coverage if not already covered.; P-04 sync optimization: Refactor sync() in meshManager.ts. Replace 'const incomingKeys = new Set(Object.keys(meshes))' and 'incomingKeys.has(key)' with direct property check: 'if (!(key in meshes))'. This avoids one Set + Array allocation per sync call while maintaining identical O(1) lookup semantics. Iterate meshMap.keys() for removal check, then Object.entries(meshes) for add/update (same as current).
Fixed resource leak in resize test by wrapping test body in try/finally so globalThis.ResizeObserver is always restored even if assertions throw. All 19 Viewport tests pass. All 13 steps complete.
